description:
**Auto-mode re-launches agents on features that already have open PRs:** 
When a feature agent completes its work and successfully creates a pull request, but the feature later gets marked as "blocked" for any transient reason (a GitHub API 502, a branch conflict, or a post-agent hook failure), auto-mode resets the feature back to the backlog. On its next cycle, auto-mode picks the feature up again, launches a brand-new agent, and that agent re-implements the entire component from scratch. When the agent finishes and tries to create a PR, it fails with "a pull request for this branch already exists" — which blocks the feature again, restarting the cycle. This was observed across seven Tier 1 features (hx-icon-button, hx-button-group, hx-number-input, hx-checkbox-group, hx-radio-group, hx-field, hx-tabs), each cycling multiple times and burning $3–16 per wasted run. The fix could maybe be a pre-flight check in the auto-mode feature picker: if a feature already has a prNumber and that PR is still open on GitHub, skip agent launch and move the feature directly to the review column instead.
